title: Creamy Mexican Beef Burrito
description: A rich, gooey meal‑prep Mexican dish featuring a velvety beef filling, creamy sauce, and fresh unwaxed lemon zest, all wrapped in warm tortillas.
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium‑high heat and brown the ground beef, breaking it up with a wooden spoon. Drain any excess fat and check that the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
Add cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, salt, and pepper; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in salsa, cooked rice, and black beans, then simmer for 3–4 minutes to combine flavors.
In a separate bowl, whisk together sour cream, mayonnaise, and lemon juice until smooth.
Pour the creamy mixture over the beef filling and stir until the filling is evenly coated.
Warm tortillas: place each on a dry skillet for about 30 seconds per side or microwave for 10 seconds.
Lay a tortilla flat on a plate, spoon 1/4 of the beef mixture onto the center.
Sprinkle 1/4 cup of shredded cheese over the filling.
Fold the sides in and roll tightly, sealing the edges. Optionally tuck in the ends for a tighter burrito.
Place the assembled burritos in a heat‑proof container for meal prep; refrigerate or reheat as desired.
